title: Objects
content:
### What are Objects?

**Objects** are collections of **key-value pairs** (properties). They store structured data.

---

### Creating Objects

```js
// Object literal (preferred)
let person = {
  name: "John",
  age: 30,
  isStudent: false
};

// Using new Object()
let car = new Object();
car.brand = "Toyota";
```

---

### Object Properties

| Part | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| **Key** | The property name | `"name"` |
| **Value** | Any data type | `"John"` |
| **Entry** | A key-value pair | `name: "John"` |

Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, other objects, or even functions.

---

### Accessing Properties

```js
let person = { name: "John", age: 30 };

// Dot notation
console.log(person.name); // "John"

// Bracket notation
console.log(person["name"]); // "John"
```

Use **bracket notation** when the key is dynamic or contains special characters:

```js
let key = "name";
console.log(person[key]); // "John"
```

---

> **Objects vs Primitives:** Objects are **reference types** -- variables store a reference, not the actual value. Primitives (string, number, boolean) are **value types**.

quiz.question: Which notation is used to access an object property?
